title: FeedConfig.poll_interval stored as String, parsed on every call
description:
LOCATION: src/config.rs:27-32, 86-93

FINDING: FeedConfig.poll_interval is a String that is re-parsed by parse_duration() on every call to FeedConfig::interval(). The parse result is discarded after validate(); the field is never converted to Duration at rest. A custom serde deserializer (or a wrapper newtype with Deserialize) would parse once, store a Duration, and remove the .expect() in interval().

IMPACT: The .expect("validated on load") in interval() is the only guard against a corruption path where a FeedConfig is constructed outside Config::load() (e.g. in tests). If poll_interval is set to an invalid string without going through validate(), interval() panics in a background tokio task, taking down that poller silently.
close_reason: Deferred: FeedConfig poll_interval parsing. The .expect() is safe since validate() runs first.
